entities.name: PEARL MILLET
entities.description: A cereal crop tested in sole and intercropped systems under high temperatures.

entities.name: COWPEA
entities.description: A legume crop tested in sole and intercropped systems under high temperatures.

entities.name: INTERCROPPING
entities.description: The practice of growing two or more crops simultaneously to reduce risk and increase resilience.

entities.name: HIGH TEMPERATURES
entities.description: Extreme heat conditions, specifically reaching up to 42.2 °C, affecting crop yield.

entities.name: SEMI-ARID INDIA
entities.description: The geographic region where the field trials were conducted.

entities.name: DRY SEASON
entities.description: The specific seasonal period during which the field experiments were performed.

entities.name: SPLIT-SPLIT-PLOT EXPERIMENT
entities.description: The experimental design used to assess the performance of sole versus intercropped stands.

entities.name: TOTAL GRAIN YIELD
entities.description: The primary productivity measure for pearl millet and cowpea.

entities.name: WATER TREATMENTS
entities.description: Different levels of irrigation (severe stress, partial stress, and well-watered) applied to crops.

entities.name: DRIP IRRIGATION
entities.description: The irrigation system used to apply specific water treatments.

entities.name: CLIMATE RESILIENT CROPPING PRACTICES
entities.description: Agricultural strategies designed to maintain production under climate extremes.

entities.name: PLANTING DENSITIES
entities.description: The spacing between rows (30 cm and 60 cm) and within rows (10 cm) for the crops.

entities.name: COWPEA GENOTYPES
entities.description: Specific genetic variations of cowpea that may be more suitable for intercropping.
